presentation.description: Server-side rendered HTML pages using Thymeleaf templates + Vanilla CSS + Vanilla JS

templates.description: Login page with form login and Google OAuth2 button
Manual registration with username, password, and role selection
Role selection page shown to new Google OAuth2 users
Complaint submission form with Leaflet map for location picking
Admin view of all complaints with worker assignment controls
Real-time GPS location history map of a specific worker
Worker view of assigned complaints with GPS auto-tracking and proof upload

frontend_libraries.name: Leaflet.js
frontend_libraries.purpose: Interactive maps for location picking and worker GPS tracking

frontend_libraries.name: OpenStreetMap
frontend_libraries.purpose: Free map tiles — no API key required

frontend_libraries.name: Vanilla JS
frontend_libraries.purpose: GPS tracking, map events, form handling, address lookup

controller.description: Spring MVC controllers — handle HTTP requests, call services, return views

routes.description: Show login page (redirect if already logged in)
Register new user with username, password, role
Show role selection page to new OAuth2 users (PENDING role only)
Save chosen role to MongoDB, update SecurityContext, redirect to dashboard
Show complaint submission form with map
Save complaint to MongoDB, redirect to success page
Show all complaints and list of workers
Assign a worker to a complaint
Show GPS location history map for a specific worker
Show complaints assigned to logged-in worker
Receive GPS coordinates from browser and save to MongoDB
Upload proof image for a completed complaint

service.description: Business logic layer — sits between controllers and repositories

classes.name: UserService
responsibilities: Register new users with hashed passwords
classes.caching: Cacheable on getAllWorkers(), CacheEvict on registerUser()

classes.name: ComplaintService
responsibilities: Assign a worker to a complaint

classes.name: WorkerLocationService
responsibilities: Save GPS coordinates for a worker
Get last N locations for a worker

classes.name: CustomUserDetailsService
responsibilities: Load user by username from MongoDB for form login
Build Spring Security UserDetails with role

classes.name: CustomOAuth2UserService
responsibilities: Called after Google verifies the user's identity
Check if email exists in MongoDB
Auto-create new user with PENDING role if first login
Return OAuth2User with authorities based on role in MongoDB

model.description: MongoDB document models (Java POJOs mapped to MongoDB collections)

classes.name: AppUser
classes.note: Implements Serializable for Redis session storage

repository.description: Spring Data MongoDB repositories — auto-implemented CRUD interfaces

classes.name: SecurityConfig
classes.description: Defines all URL access rules, form login, OAuth2 login, logout, and CSRF config

classes.name: CustomSuccessHandler
classes.description: After login, redirects user to the correct page based on role

classes.name: RedisConfig
classes.description: Configures Redis as HTTP session store and cache store

uses: HTTP session storage (@EnableRedisHttpSession) — keeps users logged in
Method-level caching (@Cacheable) — caches users and workers lists

google_oauth2.purpose: Third-party authentication — lets users sign in with their Google account

openstreetmap.purpose: Free map tiles — no API key required

nominatim.purpose: Free reverse geocoding — converts GPS coordinates to readable addresses

steps: CustomUserDetailsService loads user from MongoDB (or Redis cache)
Browser redirected to Google's login page
Google verifies credentials and returns email + name to app
CustomOAuth2UserService checks if email exists in MongoDB
New user → saved with PENDING role → redirected to /choose-role
Existing user → role loaded from MongoDB → redirected to dashboard
Session saved in Redis with authenticated user info
Worker logs in and lands on /worker/dashboard
Every 30 seconds, coordinates are POSTed to /worker/location
WorkerController saves lat/lng + timestamp to MongoDB (worker_locations)
AdminController loads last 10 locations from MongoDB
Leaflet map renders location history with markers and polyline path

flow: Citizen submits complaint (location + issue) → status: PENDING
Admin assigns a worker → status: ASSIGNED
Worker marks it cleaned → status: CLEANED

security.csrf: Disabled (spring-boot app with form login — can be re-enabled for production)
security.role_enforcement: Spring Security method-level via .hasRole() in SecurityConfig

dto_layer.description: Data Transfer Objects used to decouple controller/view from MongoDB models
